notes.note: L. Cornelius Balbus of Gades, the Spaniard whose Pompeian citizenship-grant Cicero had defended in 56 BC; in 54 BC effectively Caesar's chief of staff and Cicero's main channel for advancing Trebatius's career.

Essedum: the British (and Gallic) two-horse light war-chariot, a novelty to Roman observers in Caesar's first British expedition (55 BC) and reported on with much interest in De Bello Gallico 4. Cicero's pun is that Trebatius can ride one home if Britain has no plunder to detain him.
